facts_disclosed: Thinking about physio job in Bridgetown Barbados, nan's family there

turns.content: merry christmas or whatever is left of it lol. hope you had a good one. can i run something by you thats been sitting in my head
Merry Christmas to you too! Of course, go ahead -- what's on your mind?

right so i was going through job listings the other day yeah. not seriously just having a browse at whats out there for when i graduate. and i spotted this physio role at a community clinic in Bridgetown. as in Bridgetown Barbados
That's an interesting find. What caught your eye about it -- the location, the role itself, or both?

both tbh. its community outpatient rehab so its the kind of physio work i actually want to do. but its also Barbados which is where my nans whole family is. cousins aunties uncles everyone. ive been out to visit but ive never actually lived there. and even though i didnt grow up there it feels like home in a way init. my nan talks about Bridgetown like its the centre of the universe. probably sounds mad

Doesn't sound mad at all. A lot of people feel a deep connection to their family's home even if they didn't grow up there. The stories, the culture, the food -- it shapes you whether you've lived it firsthand or not. Have you thought about what it would actually look like if you applied?

thats where it gets complicated you know. on one hand i could be near family. actually be around my cousins instead of just FaceTiming on sundays. and the work would be meaningful. community physio out there where access is probably even worse than here. but then Bristol is my city. this is where my nan is where my mates are where everything ive built is. and shes getting older so do i really want to be on the other side of the atlantic. even though shed probably tell me to go because thats the kind of person she is
That's a real tension -- moving closer to one part of your family while moving further from the person who matters most. Have you mentioned it to your nan at all?

nah because if i tell her shell get all excited and start ringing her sisters saying marcus is coming and then its a whole thing before ive even decided anything. i havent applied. i dont even know if id get it. im just thinking out loud you know. and i cant tell if the Barbados thing is a genuine plan or if im just romanticising it because my nan makes it sound so perfect

That's an honest and mature thing to recognise. There's a difference between the dream version of Barbados and the reality of actually uprooting your life. A few practical things worth looking into quietly: would your HCPC registration transfer, what the citizenship route looks like through your nan, and whether Caribbean experience would be recognised if you came back to the UK later. You don't have to tell anyone you're researching it. Just get the facts so that when you do decide, it's based on reality.

yeah thats smart. quiet research without making it a big deal. because right now i genuinely dont know what i want man. Bristol feels like the safe choice and Barbados feels like the dream choice and i need to figure out which one is actually right for me. probably need to sit with it a bit longer tbh

There's no deadline on this decision. You're still finishing your degree so you've got time. Do the quiet research, see how you feel in the new year, and let the idea either grow legs or settle down on its own. You'll know when you're ready.

yeah alright. thanks for not telling me im being stupid because i genuinely cant tell if this is a great idea or the maddest thing ive ever thought of lol. appreciate you bruv. happy new year when it comes

Not stupid at all -- the fact that you're thinking this carefully tells me you'll make the right call when the time comes. Happy new year to you too. Enjoy the rest of the holidays and let the new year bring some clarity.
